headline: Arms Exports Raise Ethics Questions on RM1.8 Billion Sales
context: Malaysia exported RM1.8 billion in defence equipment in 2025 including to countries flagged for armed conflict involvement.

cards.big: RM1.8 billion arms exports include sales to conflict-flagged countries
cards.sub: Malaysia lacks comprehensive arms export control with human rights criteria

cards.big: Defence exports to Middle East totalled RM680 million in 2025
cards.sub: Armoured vehicles ammunition and communications shipped to 8 buyers

cards.big: Malaysia has no equivalent of EU Common Position on arms controls
cards.sub: Decisions made by Defence Ministry without mandatory rights assessment

cards.big: Civil society documented Malaysian-made vehicles in 3 conflict zones
cards.sub: Vehicles sold to intermediaries re-exported without Malaysian approval

cards.big: Arms exports test whether defence trade includes responsibility for end-use
RM1.8 billion arms exports raise ethics questions. Export policy needs criteria beyond commercial value.
